mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2024-06-01 suggests potential easing cycle ahead
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals labor market deterioration that increases recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to activate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 703088M and HOUST at 1277K as of 2024-05-01
regime_hint: demand-driven slowdown with synchronized weakness across consumption and housing
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or the joint signal produces worse predictive power than either series alone over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 12-month trend signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices earnings growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in high-INDPRO-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100k jobs per month, signaling labor market weakness that triggers a flight-to-safety rotation out of equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite weak payroll growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized rate, signaling economic slowdown that reduces corporate profit growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as terminal growth rates are repriced downward.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in low-growth periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 3% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 5%, it signals real rates are insufficiently restrictive, triggering expectations of further rate hikes that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 313.049 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2024-06-01
regime_hint: monetary policy tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or the joint signal produces worse predictive power than either series alone over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1.2 million annualized units while retail sales (RSAFS) growth slows to below 2% year-over-year, signaling synchronized demand weakness across housing and consumption that reduces forward earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1.2M annualized AND RSAFS YoY growth < 2%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1277K and RSAFS at 703088M as of 2024-05-01
regime_hint: demand-driven recession risk with housing and consumption deterioration
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or 60-day forward returns in dual-weakness periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
